title: Fix setting of version information
body:
It was setting the wrong variable (CamelCase)
in the wrong module ("main", not "libpod")...

Thus the version information was always missing,
from `podman version` and friends (e.g varlink)

comments.body:
Code LGTM
@cevich Seeing some weird Cirrus failures here

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Code LGTM @cevich Seeing some weird Cirrus failures here

Taking a look...

Ahh IC, @mheon  if you click through the cirrus links, you'll get this in a red box

```
Failed to start: The resource 'projects/libpod-218412/global/images/ubuntu-1804-bionic-v20180911-libpod-fce09afe' was not found
```

That message doesn't lie, and it's partly my fault.  After 8126eb8a merged, I purged out the old ubuntu images (there were a lot) from GCE, catching `ubuntu-...-fce09afe` along the way.  Oops.  The fix here is really easy though: Rebase the PR against master (to pick up change to ``image_name: "ubuntu-1804-bionic-v20180911-libpod-63a86a18"`` in ``.cirrus.yml``).  Make sense?  Sorry about that.

@cevich No problem, thanks for finding it!

@afbjorklund Can you rebase against master to fix the tests?
@mheon : I guess it is too late for a rebase, but which failing tests needed fixing ?

@afbjorklund We were seeing test failures on Cirrus CI caused by the Cirrus test manifest pointing to a GCE image we'd just removed (we'd built a new image for master).

It looks like those either resolved themselves, or the mergebot decided to ignore them. Hopefully the former.
